sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.113 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$H(q,c) = a,b,c,d [b,a]=[d,c], q, c$. [r]{a)} Is it true that the Schur multiplier$M(H(8,12))$has exponent 32? [r]{b)} Is it true that the Schur multiplier$M(H(7,13))$has exponent 49? The difficulty is that these groups are too big to compute using current versions of the$p$-Quotient Algorithm, which use 32 bit arithmetic. So to tackle these groups it would help to have a version of the$p$-Quot...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
